title: senecio leucanthemifolius var. humilis
intro: Senecio leucanthemifolius var. humilis, commonly known as White Dyers Weed, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family that has been used for centuries in folk medicine. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of White Dyers Weed include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to stimulate digestion. White Dyers Weed is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being.
